title: calophyllum recedens
intro: Calophyllum recedens, also known as the straight-fruit or narrow-leaved camphor tree, is a species of evergreen tree native to the tropical forests of the Pacific Islands, specifically in Fiji, Samoa, and Tonga. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly the oil extracted from its seeds, which has anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal properties. The oil has been used to treat various health issues, including skin conditions, wounds, and respiratory problems, and is believed to have antioxidant and anti-cancer properties. Traditionally, the oil is prepared through cold pressing of the seeds and used in various herbal preparations, including creams, ointments, and essential oils.
